current_wind.description: Winds are moderate from the WSW, with gusts up to 13 mph.

wind_info.description: A moderate left-to-right crosswind from the WNW. Play a hyzer flip or anhyzer.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: With an 8 o'clock crosswind from the right, this shot can expose the disc's underside. Consider a hyzer flip to control the fade, or an anhyzer line to push right. An overstable disc will be more reliable.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A good option here to keep the disc on a controlled hyzer. A stable driver should cut through the wind effectively.

wind_info.description: A strong left-to-right crosswind from the NW. This will push the disc right. Prefer a RHFH or a controlled LHBH.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Exposing the underside to a 9 o'clock crosswind is risky. Use a very overstable disc and a strong hyzer to fight the wind. A flex shot could work if you have the space.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: This is a more reliable shot, allowing the disc to cut into the wind and hyzer out. A stable driver is recommended to handle the crosswind.

wind_info.description: A direct headwind from the WSW. Use an overstable disc and aim for a straight flight.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Fight the headwind with an overstable driver. Aim for a clean, flat release to maximize distance and penetration.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An overstable driver is also a good choice here to cut through the wind.

wind_info.description: A direct tailwind from the ESE. Use an understable disc for maximum distance.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: The tailwind will help carry an understable disc. Look for a smooth release and let the wind do the work for extra distance.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An understable disc can also be used here, but be careful not to turn it over too much. A smooth release is key.

wind_info.description: A strong tailwind from the ESE. Maximize distance with an understable disc.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Maximize distance with an understable disc in this tailwind. Aim for a smooth, level throw to take advantage of the tailwind.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Similar to RHBH, an understable disc will get extra distance. Be mindful of the angle to prevent it from turning into the ground.

wind_info.description: A strong left-to-right crosswind from the NW. Will push the disc right. Prefer RHFH or controlled LHBH.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: With a 9 o'clock crosswind from the right, a stable or overstable driver on hyzer can work to fight the rightward push. An anhyzer line might also be effective.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A stable driver on hyzer is a good option to control the rightward push of the wind.

wind_info.description: A strong left-to-right crosswind from the NW. This will push the disc right. Prefer RHFH.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: With a 10 o'clock crosswind pushing right, an overstable disc on hyzer is essential. Be prepared for it to drift right.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: This is the preferred throw. A stable driver on hyzer will fight the wind effectively and shape towards the fairway.

wind_info.description: A strong tailwind from the ESE. Use an understable disc for maximum distance.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: Maximize distance with an understable disc in this tailwind. Aim for a clean, flat release.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: An understable disc will get extra distance, but be mindful of the angle to prevent it from turning over too much.

wind_info.description: A strong tailwind from the ESE. Use an understable disc for maximum distance.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: This tailwind will help carry an understable disc. Aim for a smooth, level throw for maximum distance.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: Similar to RHBH, an understable disc will get extra distance. Focus on a smooth release and control.

wind_info.description: A moderate right-to-left crosswind from the SSE. The disc will be pushed left. Consider a hyzer or anhyzer shot.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: With a 3 o'clock crosswind from the left, a stable or understable driver on hyzer can work to fight the leftward push. An anhyzer line might also be effective.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A stable driver on hyzer will help counteract the wind pushing left. A smooth release is key.

wind_info.description: A moderate right-to-left crosswind from the SSE. This will push the disc left. Prefer RHBH with hyzer or anhyzer.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: A stable driver thrown on hyzer will help fight the wind pushing left. An anhyzer line can also be effective here.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A stable driver on hyzer is a good option to control the leftward drift.

wind_info.description: A light right-to-left crosswind from the S. Will push the disc slightly left. RHBH manageable with hyzer.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: With a light 2 o'clock crosswind, a stable driver on hyzer should be manageable. The wind will provide a slight leftward push.

fh.shot_strategy: RHFH: A stable driver on hyzer can also be used here. Be mindful of the angle to keep it from turning over too much.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH: A strong 10 o'clock crosswind from the NW will push the disc right. Use a very overstable disc on a strong hyzer.
